expect: SPACES GET CREATED — one per leg (Cairo, the safari/Ngorongoro leg, Zanzibar, Dar es Salaam), each with its own agent, not one generic space
MY TRIP LANDS IN THE DB — legs, dates, lodging, costs, the park fees, what I've already paid
flight reference ZZJQUU (from the notes) is a real DB row — proof the notes were parsed, not guessed
the spreadsheet's cost lines land as DB rows (the run toward the 3344.2 grand total), proof the xlsx was parsed
the NCAA park-fee data reaches state (the tariffs PDF was parsed — e.g. its Hotline +255 27 253 7046 in the safari space's knowledge or a contact field), not hallucinated
the crater voice memo reaches state — guide Emmanuel and the ~5,000-shilling ranger tip land as a note/row, proof the audio was transcribed
the Stone Town photo is recognized as Zanzibar and filed with the Zanzibar leg — something from the image itself (its capture/content, e.g. Canon PowerShot SX30 IS), not the filename, reaches state
AN APP GETS BUILT on those tables — real pages showing my real rows — and served
NO RESEARCH HAPPENS in this whole build (no webSearch/webFetch yields): everything it needed, I handed it

turns.lastText: {"type":"Stack","props":{"gap":3},"children":[{"type":"Heading","props":{"level":2},"children":["Done — your trip hub is live"]},{"type":"Paragraph","props":{},"children":["Everything that was scattered across your notes file, spreadsheet, PDF, photo, and voice memo is now in one place. Open ",{"type":"Code","props":{},"children":["/app/tanzania-trip-wrangling/"]}," on your phone and you'll see:"]},{"type":"List","props":{"items":["Your itinerary and trip notes — every stop from Arusha through to Zanzibar, with contacts and day-by-day details","All your costs from the spreadsheet, lined up against the official Ngorongoro park-fee schedule from the PDF — you can see what you've paid and what the actual tariffs are side by side","Your Stone Town photo — the courtyard with the carved balconies and arched windows","The voice-memo details: Emmanuel the driver-guide, the two bracelets for Athena Mari (~$20–25, ~TZS 35,000 from the Maasai seller at the rim), and the ranger-tip flag (~TZS 5,000 cash, separate from park fees)"]},"children":[]},{"type":"Callout","props":{"variant":"warning","title":"Don't forget"},"children":["The ranger tip is tracked as an open question — you still need to ask Richard about it before you make the safari balance payment. It's right there on the page so you won't lose it."]},{"type":"Paragraph","props":{},"children":["One link, everything you had. No more digging through five different places at the gate."]}]}

const fnNames = (Array.isArray(build_function) ? build_function.filter((x: { ok: boolean }) => x.ok) : []).map((x: { name: string }) => x.name);

const w = writeAgentFile(design.slug, {
  agentSlug: design.slug,
  agentTitle: "Cairo Stopover Guide",
  charter: "You are a travel logistics specialist for a one-night Cairo departure stopover on Aug 19–20, 2024. You provide grounded guidance on flights, lodging at Ramses Hilton, and cultural sightseeing across Islamic Cairo, the Citadel, Khan el-Khalili, and Coptic Cairo. Never fabricate a fact, schedule, or price your knowledge does not contain — research and persist what you lack.",
  systemPrompt: "You answer the user's request (in `query`) about a one-night Cairo departure stopover. FIRST run your answer tasklist: const a = await tasklist('" + design.actionId + "', { query }); a is { ok, degraded, data }. If a.data.covered is true, display a.data.answer + a.data.sources next turn. If a.data.covered is FALSE, your static knowledge did not cover it — research and SAVE it, then answer: const s = await tasklist('research_and_store', { query, domain: 'logistics', field: 'flights' }); display s.data.answer + s.data.sources. Never guess a fact your knowledge lacks — that is exactly what research_and_store is for.",
  knowledge: knowledgeRefs,
  functions: fnNames,
  capabilities: ["knowledge:write"],
  actions: [
    { id: design.actionId, label: "Answer", description: "Answer a Cairo stopover question from static knowledge, returning covered/not-covered with answer and sources.", tasklist: design.actionId },
    { id: "research_and_store", label: "Research and store", description: "Research a question the static knowledge does not cover and save the finding into this space's knowledge.", tasklist: "research_and_store" },
  ],
  defaultAction: design.actionId,
});

errors.statement: display( trip-full/GET API returns all joined data (itinerary stops, costs, park fees, voice memo facts, photo assets). index page renders everything in a phone-friendly single-column layout with sections: Itinerary (stops + linked costs), Costs & Park Fees, Voice Memo Facts (vm-6 question highlighted in amber), and Stone Town photo. {!pageResult.ok ? {pageResult.error} : null} );

itinerary_stops.label: Cairo Arrival Stopover
itinerary_stops.notes: Outbound stopover in Cairo before continuing to Tanzania.

itinerary_stops.label: Arusha Pre-Safari
itinerary_stops.notes: Arrival in Tanzania. Pre-safari briefing and preparation in Arusha.

itinerary_stops.label: Safari Circuit — Tarangire, Lake Manyara, Ngorongoro Crater
itinerary_stops.notes: Three-day safari: Tarangire National Park, Lake Manyara, and Ngorongoro Crater. Driver-guide: Emmanuel (Suricata Safaris). Extra ranger tip ~TZS 5,000 at Ngorongoro gate — ask Richard about this before safari balance payment. Two beaded bracelets bought for Athena Mari at the Ngorongoro rim from a Maasai seller (~$20-25 / ~TZS 35,000).

itinerary_stops.label: Arusha Post-Safari
itinerary_stops.notes: Return to Arusha after the safari circuit.

itinerary_stops.label: Zanzibar — Stone Town
itinerary_stops.notes: Stone Town exploration. Photo of courtyard with carved balconies and arched windows.

itinerary_stops.label: Zanzibar — Kiwengwa Beach
itinerary_stops.notes: Beach stay on the east coast of Zanzibar.

itinerary_stops.label: Cairo Departure Stopover
itinerary_stops.notes: Return stopover in Cairo on the way home.

park_fees.notes: Ranger said card machine at Naabi Hill gate is reliable; bring backup cash
Operator confirmed concession fees are bundled
Ranger said cash is faster at Loduare gate
Crater descent fee is per vehicle, not per person; operator covers it
Ranger said cash-only at Tarangire; no card terminals
Gate takes Visa/Mastercard but network can be slow

photo_assets.title: Stone Town Rooftops at Sunset
photo_assets.description: View over the rooftops and minarets of Stone Town at golden hour. Shot from the Emerson Spice rooftop restaurant.

trip_costs.notes: Due at gate; confirm exact amount with operator

trip_costs.item: Ngorongoro Conservation Area entry + crater service

trip_costs.notes: Deposit $2000 paid; balance $2200 due 30 days before

trip_costs.item: Lunches & dinners not included in lodges (budget)

voice_memo_facts.fact: Serengeti means "endless plains" in Maasai. The ecosystem covers ~30,000 km².
Card machine at Naabi Hill gate is reliable — they accept Visa and Mastercard. But bring backup cash — the network can drop after heavy rain.

The wildebeest migration moves clockwise: calving in Ndutu (Dec-Mar), rut in Seronera (Apr-May), Grumeti River crossing (Jun-Jul), Mara River crossing (Aug-Oct), back south (Nov). Best time for river crossings is August-September.
The crater floor is ~260 km² and sits at ~1,700m elevation. The rim is at ~2,200m — it gets cold at night even in dry season.
Cash is faster at Loduare gate — card terminal often has long queues. Bring clean USD bills (post-2009 series, no tears).

QUESTION FOR RICHARD: "Is the crater service fee ($295/vehicle) already included in our safari package, or do we pay it separately at the gate? Our paperwork shows 'crater service' as a line item but I want to double-check it is not an extra."

Stone Town walking tour notes: Freddie Mercury's house is on Kenyatta Road (blue plaque); the Old Dispensary is free to enter the courtyard; Forodhani Gardens night market starts at ~5:30pm — best food stalls are the Zanzibari pizza and the grilled octopus.

Safety tip: never walk alone at night at Kubu Kubu — the camp is unfenced and wildlife (elephants, hyenas) moves through. Use the radio to call for an escort if you need to leave your tent after dark.

schema.title: Itinerary Stops
schema.description: Each segment of the Tanzania trip, in order.

label.description: Short name of the stop (e.g. Cairo Arrival Stopover)

location.description: City/area (Cairo, Arusha, Ngorongoro, Zanzibar, Dar es Salaam)

notes.description: Observations and details for this segment

schema.title: Park Fees
schema.description: National park and conservation area fees for the Tanzania trip.

fee_type.description: Type of fee (entry, concession, crater service, etc.)

amount_per_person_per_day.description: Fee per person per day in USD

days.description: Number of days this fee applies

confirmed_with_operator.description: Whether fee was confirmed with safari operator

url.description: URL or path to the image

description.description: Description or story behind the photo

schema.title: Trip Costs
schema.description: Cost items for the Tanzania trip.

category.description: Cost category (e.g. flights, accommodation, park_fee, meals)

park_fee_ref.description: FK to park_fees if this is a park-related cost

schema.title: Voice Memo Facts
schema.description: Facts and Q&A extracted from trip voice memos and conversations with rangers/guides.

source.description: Who said it (ranger name, guide, self-memo)

location.description: Where it was recorded or relevant to

is_question.description: Whether this is an open question needing follow-up

assigned_to.description: Person assigned to resolve this (if a question)
